title: Ultimate Manhunt
description: A Minecraft 1.20.1/1.21.1 Forge mod for creating, running, and managing manhunt games
body:
# **Overview:**

This is a Minecraft mod for Forge 1.20.1/1.21.1 that can be used to run a Manhunt game for you and your friends.

In a manhunt game, the hunter's goal is to eliminate speed runners, and the speed runners's goal is to beat the Ender Dragon (achieve the "Free the End" achievement). 

This mod allows speed runners to have multiple lives (1 to 5 lives), and an option to determine if lives should only be deducted for deaths by a hunter or any causes.

Using the latest version of this mod for the desired Minecraft version (1.20.1 or 1.21.1) is **highly** recommended for the best experience!

# **Laser Utils Requirements for latest versions:**

- Version 0.2.0+ for 1.21.1 - [Laser Utils 5.3.1 for 1.21.1]([https://modrinth.com/mod/laser-utils/version/5.3.1](https://modrinth.com/mod/laser-utils/version/5.3.1))
- Verison 0.0.0+ for 1.20.1 - [Laser Utils 0.1.0 for 1.20.1](https://modrinth.com/mod/laser-utils/version/0.1.0)

# **Rules:**

Speed runners can be given a head-start before the hunter starts chasing players down (duration is measured in ticks).
After a speed runner is killed by a hunter and respawns, they can be given a grace period for which they are invulnerable and untrackable to hunters in order to prevent spawn-camping. This grace period protection is not applicable if hardcore is enabled for the manhunt.
Players that join a game that is in-progress will be declared as speed runners (future update planned to allow players to join as hunters or spectators).
Speed runners that are defeated by hunters will become hunters (future update planned to allow the option of becoming a spectator).
There is also the option for speed runners to recieve a knockback item to help keep hunters away (known as the "Wind Torch"). The Wind Torch has 50 uses, and cannot be repaired.

# **Player Tracking:**

Every manhunt game requires a player tracker for hunters to use to find speed runners. I've decided to go a different route from the traditional player-tracking compass, and make something that sets this mod apart from the others.

While there is no need for a player-tracking compass mod, you could use one if desired. This mod features a tracker for hunters that renders an arrow on the screen and points in the direction of a speed runner (can cycle through available players using the left and right arrow keys by default). Distance between the hunter and speed runner is also tracked.

This tracker does not track players that aren't in the same dimension as the hunter, nor does it track speed runners that are on grace period protection. If the player tracker stops working (ex: when a player should be trackable but isn't selectable), or is having difficulties, hunters can use the "resetPlayerTracker" command or press 'G' to reset the tracker.

If you wish to not use the built-in player tracker, you can disable it by pressing 'M' to go to client settings, go to the last page, and click to disable the tracker (can be re-enabled if needed!).

There is also a total count of remaining hunters/speed runners at the top of the screen.

Link to video demonstrating the [player tracker](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3UpBFQ9-RoM)

# **Hunters:**

There is an option to assign a hunter as a "buffed" hunter. Buffed hunters have the following buffs:

- +5 base armor points
- +50% higher base health
- +25% higher base attack damage
- +10% higher base movement speed
- +10% movement efficiency (1.21.1)
- +10% water movement efficiency (1.21.1)
- +15% mining efficiency (1.21.1)
- +15% submerged mining efficiency (1.21.1)

All buffed hunters are also granted permanent saturation, and are able to use the built-in player tracker.

# **Speed Runners:**

Speed runners are alerted when hunters get within 50 blocks of them. Their screen will get a red tint around the edges, and heart-beating sounds will play. If music is enabled, the Prowler theme will play.

# **Commands:**

As of version 0.2.0 for 1.21.1, and version 0.0.0 for 1.20.1, all commands for managing game settings have been reimplemented as arguments for a singular command.

All game settings, etc. for managing your Manhunt game can be reached using "/ultimate_manhunt {args}". This command requires at least OP permission level 2.
